questions.question_text: In the picture on the right you see a map. In the middle a piece is missing. The cat should be able to reach the milk, and the mouse the cheese, but the cat and the mouse must not meet each other. What should the piece in the middle look like?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: A 40 minute long lesson began at 11:50 hours. Exactly in the middle of the lesson a bird flew into the classroom. At what time did this happen?
questions.solution: Lesson starts 11:50, lasts 40 minutes. Middle = 11:50 + 20 min = 12:10. Answer: 12:10.
solution_steps: Lesson starts at 11:50 and lasts 40 minutes.

questions.question_text: Which square contains 3 quadrilaterals, 3 circles and 4 hearts?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: In a cafe the soup costs € 4, the main course € 9 and the dessert € 5. The three courses when ordered together cost € 15. How many Euro’s cheaper is this, than ordering the same three courses separately?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for this question. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Six coins build a triangle (see picture). What is the smallest number of coins that must be moved to create the circle?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Four friends each eat some icecream. Mike eats more than Franz, Jaroslav eats more than Veit and Jaroslav eats less than Franz. Put the friends in order according to how much icecream they have eaten. Begin with the largest amount.
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for this question. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: I have tiles that look like this... Which pattern can I not create with them?
questions.solution: See PDF page 1 for the figure. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Eva is a centipede with exactly 100 feet. Yesterday she bought 16 pairs of shoes and wore them straight away. Even so she still had 14 feet with no shoes. On how many feet did she already wear shoes before going shopping yesterday?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Given that ▲ + ▲ + 6 = ▲ + ▲ + ▲ + ▲. Which number should replace ▲?
Maria folds a square piece of paper in such a way that the kangaroos exactly overlap each other. Along how many of the lines shown is this possible?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Matthias and Klara live in a tower block. Klara lives 12 floors above Matthias. One day Matthias climbs the staircase to visit Klara. When halfway he is on the 8th floor. On which floor does Klara live?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: A large cube is made from 64 small cubes. The 5 visible faces of the large cube are green, the bottom face is red. How many of the small cubes have 3 green faces?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Kangi walks directly from the zoo to school (Schule) and counts the flowers along the way. Which of the following numbers can he not obtain this way?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: A ferry boat can transport, in one journey, either 10 cars or 6 lorries. Yesterday the boat crossed the river 5 times. It was always fully loaded and transported a total of 42 vehicles. How many of these were cars?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: C.

questions.question_text: Hans began a chain-email. He sent an e-mail to his friend Peter, who sent it on to 2 more people. Each person that receives the e-mail should send it to 2 more people. After 3 rounds 1+2+4 = 7 people have received the e-mail. How many people have received the e-mail after 5 rounds?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: On the playground some children measure the length of the playground with their strides. Anni makes 15 Strides, Betty 17, Denis 12 und Ivo 14. Who has the longest stride?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: A.

questions.question_text: Which number must replace the questionmark, if the total of the numbers in each row is the same?
questions.solution: See PDF page 2 for this question. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: The number 60 × 60 × 24 × 7 is the same as

options.A: The number of minutes in seven weeks
options.B: The number of hours in sixty days
options.C: The number of seconds in seven hours
options.D: The number of seconds in one week
options.E: The number of minutes in 24 weeks

questions.solution: 60 × 60 × 24 × 7: compute step by step. 60 × 60 = 3600 (seconds per hour). 3600 × 24 = 86400 (seconds per day). 86400 × 7 = 604800 (seconds per week). This equals the number of seconds in 7 days = 1 week. Answer: number of seconds in one week.
solution_steps: Answer: the number of seconds in one week.

questions.question_text: Two years ago the cats Tim and Tom were together 15 years old. Now Tom is 13 years old. In how many years will Tim be 9 years old?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Kamilla wrote down all of the numbers from 1- 100 one after the other in a table with 5 columns. A part of the table is shown. Her brother cut out a piece of the table and erased some of the numbers. Which of the following could this piece have been?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: The teacher said „in our school library there are roughly 2010 books.“ The pupils then guessed exactly how many there are. Artur guesses 2010, Beate 1998 und Carlos 2015. They are wrong by 12, 7 and 5, but not in this order. How many books are in the library?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: E.

questions.question_text: lines are drawn on a piece of paper and some of the lines are given numbers. The paper is cut along some of these lines and then folded as shown in the picture. What is the total of the numbers on the lines that were cut?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for the figure. Correct answer: B.

questions.question_text: Andrew, Stefan, Robert and Marko meet each other at a concert in Zagreb. They come from different cities: Paris, Dubrovnik, Rome and Berlin (not necessarily in this order) - Andrew and the friend from Berlin arrive first in Zagreb. Neither of the two have been to Paris or Rome - Robert is not from Berlin, but he arrives with the friend from Paris. - Marko and the friend from Paris enjoyed the concert very much. From which city does Marko come?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: D.

questions.question_text: Berti’s friends add together the day and the month of their birthdays. They each get the answer 35, but no two have the same birthday. What is the maximum number of friends Berti has?
questions.solution: See PDF page 3 for this question. Correct answer: E.
